roadmap.goal: Implement the platform's built-in X.509 Certificate Authority (GH #28): a persistent 3-tier trust hierarchy (root -> per-node intermediate pathLen=0 -> workload SVID with a single SPIFFE URI SAN) behind a Ca port trait, with the root key envelope-encrypted at rest (HKDF-SHA256 -> AES-256-GCM, KEK in kernel keyring via systemd-creds), an issued_certificates audit row, and DST-deterministic issuance. Turn the 39 RED scaffolds GREEN in trust-hierarchy dependency order.
notes: TDD: every step turns one or more of the 39 authored RED scaffolds GREEN. The scaffolds (#[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]) are the executable acceptance SSOT; distill/test-scenarios.md is the GIVEN/WHEN/THEN companion. GREEN replaces each panic! body with real assertions (L1/L2 default-lane) or real crypto/keyring calls (L3 integration-tests, Lima).

TECH STATE (do NOT plan a bump step): rcgen 0.14.8 (features = ["ring", "pem"], MSRV 1.88) is ALREADY committed (35958ecb); mint_ephemeral_ca is ALREADY migrated to the 0.14 Issuer API (Issuer::from_params(&params, &key) + 2-arg signed_by(&key, &issuer)). Crypto provider = ring (ECDSA P-256 + AES-256-GCM + HKDF-SHA256). aws-lc-rs + FIPS 140-3 Cert #4816 are deferred to #204. RcgenCa only confirms IsCa::Ca(BasicConstraints::Constrained(0)) / SanType::URI(Ia5String) / KeyUsagePurpose at first compile (all present in 0.14.8).

REUSE (HARD GATE): REUSE-AS-IS IntentStore, ObservationStore, Entropy, SpiffeId/CertSerial/NodeId (overdrive-core/src/id.rs), VersionedEnvelope/codec::envelope. CREATE-NEW (the 8): Ca trait, CertSpec, RcgenCa, SimCa, Kek port, RootCaKeyEnvelope, IssuedCertificateRow(Envelope), the AEAD codec. tls_bootstrap.rs is LEAVE-AS-IS (distinct consumer = control-plane HTTPS; do NOT touch beyond the already-done rcgen migration).

DECISIONS honored (ADR-0063 D1-D8 / DES-CA-1..10): Ca trait speaks project newtypes + typed PEM/DER byte newtypes, never rcgen types; root key = rkyv RootCaKeyEnvelope in IntentStore (intent, never observation); KEK in kernel keyring via systemd-creds; HKDF-from-KEK subkey -> AES-256-GCM (AAD = kek_id); pure CertSpec in core (host translates to rcgen::CertificateParams); serials via Entropy (key-gen via backend CSPRNG, not injectable); issued_certificates ObservationStore audit row; refuse-to-start (typed CaError + health.startup.refused) on decrypt/KEK-probe failure, never silent re-mint; single-node = exactly one intermediate; Earned-Trust probe (KEK-present + envelope-decrypt + credential-present) is wire->probe->use at the composition root.

WALKING SKELETON: realised across Slices 01->04 (root -> persist -> intermediate -> SVID -> full chain verifies). The single @walking_skeleton scenario is S-04-07 (rcgen_full_svid_chain_verifies_root_intermediate_svid), landed in step 04-03 -- the completion point of the skeleton, before the Slice-05 enhancements (the project-defined 'early' relative to Release 1).

DELIVER HANDOFF NOTE 1 (@error 40% bar): the 39 scaffolds carry 15 @error scenarios = 38.5%, one short of the BDD 40% target. To clear it WITHOUT new files, DELIVER MAY add one @error scenario to ca_cert_spec_policy.rs -- a wrong-trust-domain SpiffeId rejection (SVID request whose SpiffeId is outside the trust domain -> CertSpecError, distinct from InvalidSan). Attach to step 04-01 if taken. OPTIONAL and at DELIVER/reviewer discretion -- the eight scaffold files are the authored DISTILL SSOT; this is an additive in-file scenario, not a scaffold rewrite. If added, it is NOT one of the 39 mapped scenarios.

DELIVER HANDOFF NOTE 2 (trust_bundle wire-format): pin trust_bundle() to ADR-0063 D1 -- TrustBundle = root anchor + intermediate as untrusted chain material (typed byte newtype, e.g. TrustBundlePem). Cross-adapter mixing is NOT asserted (host and sim have different roots by construction); each adapter's own leaf verifies against its own bundle. Pinned in step 05-02 (S-05-02).

DELIVER HANDOFF NOTE 3 (no rcgen bump step): per TECH STATE above -- the DISTILL/ADR-0063-Consequences 'bump 0.13.2 -> 0.14.8 first-compile gate' framing is STALE; the bump and mint_ephemeral_ca migration are already committed. No step bumps rcgen.

OBSERVED STALENESS (orchestrator action): ADR-0063 § Consequences still states 'Target is rcgen 0.14.8; the workspace currently pins 0.13.2 -- the bump is a DELIVER first-compile gate' and the DISTILL handoff (feature-delta.md) still says 'bump rcgen 0.13.2 -> 0.14.8'. These are now factually wrong (rcgen 0.14 + Issuer-API migration committed in 35958ecb). The feature-delta DESIGN sections were corrected this pass; the ADR Consequences + DISTILL handoff were OUT of the architect's instructed edit scope (DESIGN-sections-only). Surfaced for the orchestrator to decide whether to correct the ADR/DISTILL via the architect.

TEST DISCIPLINE: L1 (overdrive-core CertSpec policy + schema_evolution golden-bytes) and L2 (overdrive-sim SimCa) run in the DEFAULT lane (no integration-tests feature). L3 (overdrive-host RcgenCa crypto + openssl verify; overdrive-control-plane boot/issuance/equivalence) are gated behind the integration-tests feature and run via Lima (cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run ... --features integration-tests). The macOS --no-run compile gate is necessary but not sufficient for L3.

PARADIGM: this is a Rust workspace following object-oriented ports-and-adapters (@nw-software-crafter). The Hypothesis/assert_state_delta TEST PARADIGM MANDATE in the nw-roadmap skill is Python-specific and does NOT apply -- the project test discipline (.claude/rules/testing.md) is the authority: proptest for PBT (S-04-01/02 single-URI-SAN are PBT-full at L1), rkyv golden-bytes for schema evolution, example-based sad paths at L3, the ca_equivalence DST contract test as the structural guard. See feature-delta.md DISTILL § Mandate Compliance.

DEFERRALS cite real issues only: #40 (rotation lifecycle, needs #39 workflow primitive), #36 (multi-node per-node intermediates + node attestation), #204 (aws-lc-rs switch + FIPS), #39 (workflow primitive). No invented numbers.

phases.name: Slice 01 - Root CA behind the Ca port trait
phases.description: US-CA-01: rcgen + ring mint a SPIFFE-hierarchy self-signed P-256 root behind the Ca port trait, DST-deterministic via the sim adapter, openssl-verifiable via the host adapter. Lands the cross-cutting foundation (pure CertSpec policy, the Ca trait, CaError, handle/material newtypes) that every later slice builds on.

steps.name: Core CertSpec sum type, root profile, error taxonomy
criteria: CertRole sum type { Root, Intermediate { path_len }, Svid } exists in overdrive-core; invalid role/extension combinations are unrepresentable.
CertSpec::root() yields a profile that is CA:TRUE, carries keyCertSign and cRLSign, marks keyUsage critical, carries NO pathLen, and subject is the trust domain only (no path component).
CertSpecError variants are distinct per failure mode; an invalid SAN cardinality surfaces CertSpecError::InvalidSan, NOT a flattened Internal(String) catch-all.
overdrive-core stays dst-lint-clean (crate_class = core; no rcgen/ring/rand on its compile path).
steps.implementation_notes: Pure L1 policy, default lane. Foundation of the feature: build the CertRole sum type + CertSpec builder + CertSpecError ONCE here; later slices (03-01 intermediate, 04-01 SVID) extend the same cert_spec.rs with the other role profiles -- this is per-slice extension forced by the slice-grouping mandate, NOT batchable into one step (each role carries distinct extensions/invariants). Per development.md type-driven design: make invalid states unrepresentable. CertSpecError per development.md 'Distinct failure modes get distinct error variants'. S-01-05 carries both @S-01 and @S-04 (cross-role error-taxonomy guard); it lands here with the error enum. DELIVER HANDOFF NOTE 1: if clearing the 40% @error bar, the wrong-trust-domain SpiffeId rejection scenario is a natural in-file addition here or at 04-01 (optional). Reuse SpiffeId/CertSerial from overdrive-core/src/id.rs as-is. PARADIGM: object-oriented Rust; the @error variant-distinctness is an example-based assertion, not PBT (PBT-full is reserved for the single-URI-SAN property at 04-01).

steps.name: Ca trait, CaError, handles, plus SimCa root issuance
criteria: Ca trait (root / issue_intermediate / issue_svid / trust_bundle) exists in overdrive-core/src/traits/ca.rs speaking project newtypes + typed PEM/DER byte newtypes, never rcgen types; every method rustdoc pins preconditions/postconditions/edge-cases/observable-invariants.
CaError and the handle/material newtypes (RootCaHandle, IntermediateHandle, SvidMaterial, SvidRequest, TrustBundle) exist with contract-observable accessors.
SimCa (overdrive-sim, adapter-sim) implements Ca::root() loading a fixture P-256 key; root() is bit-identical across two runs at the same seed (0x5EED).
SimCa takes its dependencies (fixture key, Kek, Entropy) as required constructor parameters, never defaulted.
steps.implementation_notes: The Ca trait + CaError + handles ride with the first adapter that implements them (SimCa root). Trait-contract rigor per development.md 'Trait definitions specify behavior, not just signature' -- pin edge cases (empty SAN -> CaError::InvalidSan, re-issue distinct serial, pathLen=0 enforced, trust_bundle composition) even though they are exercised in later steps. Per ADR-0063 D1 the trait surface speaks newtypes only (CaCertPem/SvidMaterial/TrustBundlePem) so rcgen never reaches core. SimCa fixture key loaded via PEM (key-gen is NOT injectable -- research F11/ADR-0063 D7); serials via SeededEntropy through the existing Entropy port. Port-trait dependency discipline (development.md): constructor params required, no with_clock-style builder, no production-binding default. overdrive-sim owns turmoil/StdRng; the fixture key + SimCa stay in adapter-sim. PARADIGM: example-based L2 determinism (byte-equality across two same-seed runs), not PBT.

steps.name: RcgenCa root self-sign plus root profile equivalence
criteria: RcgenCa (overdrive-host, adapter-host) implements Ca::root(): translates CertSpec::root() to rcgen::CertificateParams and self-signs a P-256 root via the ring backend.
openssl verify -CAfile root.pem root.pem exits 0; x509-parser confirms CA:TRUE, keyCertSign, keyUsage critical on the real cert bytes.

The contract-observable root profile (is_ca, key_usages, key_usage_critical, subject) is equivalent across RcgenCa (host) and SimCa (sim) via the trait accessors (key/serial bytes excluded -- sim fixture key differs by construction).
All rcgen/ring usage stays inside overdrive-host; overdrive-core compile path remains rcgen/ring-free.
steps.implementation_notes: L3, gated integration-tests, run via Lima. The root equivalence scenario (S-01-03) folds in here because this is the first step where BOTH adapters implement root() -- attaching it to the host step (rather than a standalone test-only step) keeps it from being validation-only and makes the equivalence the natural acceptance of the host adapter. The equivalence harness lives in overdrive-control-plane (the ONLY crate that dev-deps both overdrive-host and overdrive-sim -- the sim/host split is load-bearing per CLAUDE.md). NO rcgen bump: reuse the already-migrated 0.14 Issuer-API pattern from tls_bootstrap.rs::mint_ephemeral_ca (Issuer::from_params + 2-arg signed_by; self_signed for the root) -- but do NOT touch tls_bootstrap.rs (distinct consumer). Confirm SanType::URI(Ia5String) / IsCa / KeyUsagePurpose against 0.14.8 at first compile. openssl verify is a real subprocess per testing.md Tier 3 (assert on observable kernel/tool output, not internal reachability).

phases.name: Slice 02 - Root CA key envelope-encrypted at rest
phases.description: US-CA-02: the root survives restart with its key protected by authenticated encryption (HKDF-SHA256 -> AES-256-GCM via ring), KEK in the kernel keyring delivered by systemd-creds. Decrypt/KEK-probe failure refuses startup (typed CaError + health.startup.refused), never silently re-mints. Guardrail K3: zero plaintext key bytes at rest.

steps.name: RootCaKeyEnvelope rkyv versioned envelope and golden bytes
criteria: RootCaKeyRecordV1 payload + codec-internal RootCaKeyEnvelope { V1(..) } exist per ADR-0048: public alias-to-payload (pub type RootCaKeyRecord = RootCaKeyRecordV1), envelope NOT re-exported from lib.rs, writes go through RootCaKeyEnvelope::latest(..).
RootCaKeyRecordV1 persists inputs not derived state: kek_id, salt, info, nonce, ciphertext, aead_tag (the AEAD envelope fields) -- never a decoded key.
FIXTURE_V1 golden bytes round-trip: hex-decode -> rkyv-deserialise -> into_latest() equals the canonical Latest projection; discriminant_offset_from_end() agrees with an independent pin.
An unknown/forward envelope version surfaces EnvelopeError via probe_known_variant (intent fail-fast), not a garbage decode.
steps.implementation_notes: L1 archive, default lane (pure rkyv, no I/O). Reuse VersionedEnvelope / codec::envelope / EnvelopeError / probe_known_variant as-is from overdrive-core/src/codec/envelope.rs. Per ADR-0048 + testing.md 'Archive schema-evolution roundtrip': existing FIXTURE_V1 literals are NEVER touched once minted; adding V2 later adds a new fixture + assertion in one commit. The typed codec (RootCaKeyRecord::archive_for_store / from_store_bytes) co-locates on the payload per ADR-0048 §4b; decode failure on the intent path emits health.startup.refused + IntentStoreError::Envelope (intent fail-fast -- asymmetric vs observation which logs-and-skips). dst-lint-clean (core). The discriminant-offset two-source pin is the structural defense against silent layout drift; see user memory feedback_rkyv_envelope_forward_traps for the known forward gaps to avoid.

steps.name: Kek port plus HKDF to AES-256-GCM root-key AEAD codec
criteria: Kek provider port exists in overdrive-core (newtype KekId; provider yields raw 256-bit KEK bytes); no keyring/FFI in core.

The host AEAD codec seals the root key: HKDF-SHA256 derive a per-use subkey from the KEK (salt + info = overdrive/ca/root-key/v1), then AES-256-GCM-Seal with AAD = kek_id; open round-trips to byte-identical key DER under the same KEK.
The serialized RootCaKeyRecord (and its IntentStore blob) byte-scanned for the plaintext key DER finds ZERO occurrences (KPI K3, fail-closed).
A tampered ciphertext fails open with a distinct corrupt/tampered-envelope CaError; a wrong KEK fails with a distinct wrong-KEK CaError (the GCM auth tag + AAD distinguish them).
steps.implementation_notes: L3, gated integration-tests, Lima. ADR-0063 D4 (reconciliation A): HKDF-from-KEK over direct-AEAD buys domain separation (vary info) + a rotation seam (kek_id + per-seal salt) for #40/HSM at negligible cost. All HKDF-SHA256 + AES-256-GCM via ring (the workspace provider; aws-lc-rs/FIPS -> #204). The Kek PORT is core (pure, dst-lint-clean); the AEAD codec is host (ring FFI). Distinct error variants per development.md 'Distinct failure modes get distinct error variants' -- tampered vs wrong-KEK is an AC (AEAD auth tag distinguishes; AAD = kek_id defends KEK-confusion). K3 byte-scan is fail-closed: any plaintext-key occurrence fails. Persist inputs not derived state (development.md): store ciphertext + AEAD params, recompute (decrypt) on read.

steps.name: SystemdCredsKeyring Kek plus CA boot probe and refuse-to-start
criteria: SystemdCredsKeyring (overdrive-host) implements the Kek port: systemd-creds LoadCredentialEncrypted -> kernel keyring (add_key/keyctl); the dev-only OVERDRIVE_CA_KEK fallback is gated and logged, refused in production unless explicitly opted in.
Control-plane boot wiring composes the CA, persists the envelope-encrypted root on first boot, and on subsequent boot decrypts and reuses the SAME root identity (same public key) across restart.
Earned-Trust probe (wire->probe->use) at the composition root: a persisted envelope that cannot be decrypted refuses startup with a typed CaError + health.startup.refused and mints NO new root.
An absent keyring KEK (and no dev opt-in) refuses startup before any issuance and generates NO throwaway KEK.
steps.implementation_notes: L3, gated integration-tests, Lima. ADR-0063 D3 + D8 Earned-Trust: the boot path probes (a) KEK present in keyring (keyctl/add_key round-trip) and (b) the persisted envelope decrypts (trial HKDF-derive + AES-GCM-open) BEFORE the control plane accepts traffic; failure refuses startup, never silent re-mint (a re-mint orphans every issued identity). Refuse-to-start is the composition-root invariant. RootCaKeyEnvelope persistence uses the existing IntentStore (LocalStore over redb) as-is -- CA material is intent, never observation (whitepaper §4). The exact control-plane file (ca_boot.rs vs extending the existing serve/bootstrap path) is a crafter HOW-decision; create a focused module rather than bloating an existing handler. Cgroup-free boot path -- the refuse-to-start scenarios assert startup outcome + health.startup.refused + absence of a newly-minted root (fail-closed universe). systemd-creds + kernel keyring are Linux production ABIs probed via Earned-Trust (not network services -> no consumer-driven contract tests needed). Tests run as root via cargo xtask lima run --.

phases.name: Slice 03 - Per-node intermediate CA, pathLen-constrained
phases.description: US-CA-03: the platform issues a pathLen=0 node intermediate signed by the root, bounding node-compromise blast radius. Single-node = exactly one intermediate. pathLen=0 is enforced (a chain where the intermediate signs a further CA fails verification), not merely set. Signing failure fails node bootstrap loudly.

steps.name: Core CertSpec intermediate profile pathLen zero
criteria: CertSpec::intermediate() yields a profile that is CA:TRUE with pathLenConstraint=0 (CertRole::Intermediate { path_len: 0 }), carries keyCertSign, and marks keyUsage critical.
The profile is pure core policy (dst-lint-clean); the sum type makes the pathLen an explicit field, not a sentinel.
The intermediate profile shares the same CertSpec surface the host and sim adapters consume (no divergent shortcut).
steps.implementation_notes: L1 pure, default lane. Extends the cert_spec.rs sum type from 01-01 with the Intermediate role profile -- a distinct role with distinct extensions (CA:TRUE + pathLen=0 + keyCertSign), placed in this slice per the slice-grouping mandate (NOT a batch-duplicate of 01-01/04-01; each role profile is a separate invariant). Per ADR-0063 D5 the pathLen lives in core policy so it is DST-testable and shared by both adapters. The 0.14.8 mapping is IsCa::Ca(BasicConstraints::Constrained(0)) (confirmed present) -- but that translation is the host adapter's job (03-03), not core's. PARADIGM: example-based profile assertion.

steps.name: SimCa intermediate issuance chains to fixture root
criteria: SimCa::issue_intermediate(&node) issues a deterministic intermediate: both material and serial are byte-identical across two runs at the same seed.
The issued intermediate chains to the fixture root (issuer linkage observable via the trait accessor).
Serial is drawn via SeededEntropy through the existing Entropy port.
steps.implementation_notes: L2 sim, default lane. Extends SimCa (from 01-02) with issue_intermediate. Deterministic = serial via SeededEntropy + fixture intermediate key loaded via PEM (key-gen not injectable). Chains-to-fixture-root is an observable linkage (the intermediate's issuer == the fixture root's subject/serial). Per CLAUDE.md ordered-collection discipline and testing.md K3 seed->bit-identical: the two-run byte-equality is the determinism assertion. PARADIGM: example-based L2 determinism.

steps.name: RcgenCa intermediate signed-by root, pathLen enforced, equivalence
criteria: RcgenCa::issue_intermediate signs an intermediate by the root (rcgen Issuer::from_params + 2-arg signed_by), CA:TRUE, pathLenConstraint=0, keyCertSign, keyUsage critical.
openssl verify -CAfile root.pem intermediate.pem exits 0; x509-parser confirms CA:TRUE, pathLen=0, keyCertSign, keyUsage critical on real bytes.
pathLen=0 is ENFORCED not merely set: a constructed root -> intermediate -> further-CA chain fails openssl verification (non-zero exit).
The intermediate contract-observable profile (is_ca, path_len, chains_to_root, key_usages) is equivalent across host and sim adapters.
steps.implementation_notes: L3, gated integration-tests, Lima. Extends RcgenCa (from 01-03) with issue_intermediate; intermediate equivalence (S-03-03) folds in here (both adapters now implement issue_intermediate). pathLen=0 ENFORCEMENT (S-03-05) is the load-bearing sad path: build a real root->intermediate->further-CA chain and assert openssl verify FAILS -- proves the constraint is honored by the verifier, not just present in the cert (research Finding 4). Reuse the 0.14 signed_by pattern from mint_ephemeral_ca (do NOT touch tls_bootstrap.rs). IsCa::Ca(BasicConstraints::Constrained(0)) is the 0.14.8 translation. openssl verify is a real subprocess (Tier 3: assert on tool exit/output, not internal reachability).

steps.name: Node-bootstrap intermediate issuance fails loudly on signing failure
criteria: Node-bootstrap wiring issues the single node intermediate at bootstrap (single-node = one node -> one intermediate).
When the root key is unavailable (decrypt failed upstream), issue_intermediate surfaces a typed CaError and node bootstrap fails loudly.
No half-provisioned workload state is left behind on intermediate-signing failure.
steps.implementation_notes: L3, gated integration-tests, Lima. Wires intermediate issuance into the node-bootstrap path (single-node: one intermediate, no node-registration verb -- multi-node per-node intermediates + node attestation are #36). Loud-fail per ADR-0063: a signing failure (root key unavailable) surfaces a typed CaError; the node does not run workloads it cannot identify, and leaves no half-provisioned state (reconciler-discipline: no adopt-and-skip of a partial). Extends the ca_boot.rs module from 02-03. Universe: node-bootstrap outcome (fails loudly) + absence of half-provisioned state (fail-closed). Example-based sad path (Mandate 11).

phases.name: Slice 04 - Workload SVID with single SPIFFE URI SAN (walking skeleton)
phases.description: US-CA-04: mint a SPIFFE-spec-compliant SVID (exactly one spiffe:// URI SAN, CA:FALSE, keyUsage=digitalSignature critical, ~1h TTL, CSPRNG serial via Entropy) that validates through the full 3-tier chain. COMPLETES THE WALKING SKELETON (S-04-07: full Root->Intermediate->SVID chain verifies under openssl verify). KPI K2: the single-URI-SAN invariant is enforced (0 or >=2 rejected pre-issuance), DST-deterministic serials (K5).

steps.name: Core CertSpec SVID profile and single-URI-SAN invariant (PBT)
criteria: CertSpec::svid(..) yields a leaf: CA:FALSE, exactly one URI SAN equal to the requested SpiffeId, keyUsage=digitalSignature critical, NO keyCertSign/cRLSign.
For any SpiffeId yielding exactly one spiffe:// URI SAN the profile is a spec-compliant leaf (proptest property over a SpiffeId strategy).
For any SAN projection yielding zero or two-or-more URI SANs, CertSpec::svid returns Err(CertSpecError::InvalidSan) before any partial spec escapes (proptest property, KPI K2 negative).
The sole URI SAN preserves the SpiffeId newtype's canonical-lowercase form exactly (e.g. spiffe://overdrive.local/job/payments/alloc/a1b2c3).
steps.implementation_notes: L1 pure, default lane, PBT-FULL at GREEN. The single-URI-SAN invariant (KPI K2) is the highest-value invariant in the feature -- per ADR-0063 D5 it lives in core (CertSpec::svid precondition), so it is DST-testable and dst-lint-clean. PARADIGM (mandatory here, the one PBT-full step): S-04-01 and S-04-02 are proptest! properties -- S-04-01 over a SpiffeId strategy yielding exactly one spiffe:// URI; S-04-02 over a strategy generating 0 and >=2 URI-SAN inputs, asserting Err(CertSpecError::InvalidSan) for every case with no partial spec escaping (Hebert ch.6 negative testing). S-04-03 is the example-pinned readability companion. Reuse SpiffeId (overdrive-core/src/id.rs) + its Arbitrary impl if present (generators live next to the types per testing.md); if absent, add the strategy. DELIVER HANDOFF NOTE 1: the optional wrong-trust-domain SpiffeId rejection @error scenario (to clear the 40% bar) is a natural addition to this file.

steps.name: SimCa SVID issuance, deterministic 64-bit serial, single URI SAN
criteria: SimCa::issue_svid(&req) mints an SVID whose serial (drawn via SeededEntropy::fill) is byte-identical across two runs at seed 0x5EED.
Each serial is at least 64 bits wide (CA/B Forum floor, research Finding 10).
The sim SVID carries exactly one URI SAN equal to the SpiffeId and is CA:FALSE (shares the core CertSpec policy, no divergent sim shortcut).
steps.implementation_notes: L2 sim, default lane. Extends SimCa (from 01-02) with issue_svid -- consuming the SAME CertSpec::svid policy from 04-01 (proving the sim does not diverge on the single-URI-SAN rule). Serial via SeededEntropy through the Entropy port -> DST-deterministic (two same-seed runs byte-identical); >=64-bit width assertion (serial.len()*8 >= 64). Fixture leaf signed by the fixture intermediate. PARADIGM: example-based L2 determinism + profile assertion (the generative single-URI-SAN PROPERTY is at L1/04-01; L2 asserts the sim honors it for the example SpiffeId).

steps.name: RcgenCa SVID, full chain verifies (walking skeleton), leaf profile, bad-SAN reject
criteria: RcgenCa::issue_svid mints a real SVID (serial via Entropy) signed by the node intermediate; WALKING SKELETON: openssl verify -CAfile root.pem -untrusted intermediate.pem svid.pem exits 0.
The real SVID leaf carries exactly one URI SAN equal to the SpiffeId, CA:FALSE, keyUsage=digitalSignature critical, NO keyCertSign/cRLSign, validity ~1h (x509-parser on real bytes).
A SvidRequest whose SpiffeId would yield zero or >=2 URI SANs is rejected with CaError::InvalidSan before producing any certificate bytes (the core guard fires in the host adapter).
All rcgen/ring usage stays in overdrive-host.
steps.implementation_notes: L3, gated integration-tests, Lima. THE WALKING-SKELETON COMPLETION (S-04-07, @walking_skeleton): the headline end-to-end proof -- a real workload SVID chain-verifies Root->Intermediate->SVID via openssl verify -CAfile root.pem -untrusted intermediate.pem svid.pem -> exit 0. Litmus (Dim 5): Sam runs openssl verify himself and confirms the workload identity validates to the root. Requires the full chain from prior steps (root 01-03, intermediate 03-03). serial via the Entropy port (OsEntropy in prod). SVID = SanType::URI(Ia5String) single SAN, CA:FALSE, keyUsage=digitalSignature critical, ~1h TTL (research Finding 6). Bad-SAN rejection (S-04-09): the CertSpec::svid core guard fires inside the host adapter -> CaError::InvalidSan before any cert bytes. No operator CLI verb (D-CA-4) -- openssl verify is the honest external entry point. NO rcgen bump (already 0.14.8).

steps.name: SVID and bad-SAN equivalence across host and sim adapters
criteria: The SVID contract-observable profile (is_ca, san_uris cardinality+value, key_usages, issuer_linkage) is equivalent across RcgenCa (host) and SimCa (sim) for the same SpiffeId; both leaves are CA:FALSE with exactly one URI SAN and keyUsage=digitalSignature critical.
A bad-SAN request (SpiffeId yielding 0 or >=2 URI SANs) is rejected identically by both adapters with the SAME CaError::InvalidSan before any cert (the policy lives in core, not the adapter).
The SvidMaterial / SVID-handle contract-observable accessor surface (san_uris, is_ca, key_usages, issuer_linkage) is finalized on the Ca trait types.
steps.implementation_notes: L3, gated integration-tests, Lima. The SVID equivalence is the highest-value structural guard in the feature (development.md 'The DST equivalence test is the structural guard') -- it proves the sim adapter does not diverge on the K2 single-URI-SAN policy from the host adapter. NOT validation-only: this step finalizes the SvidMaterial / SVID-handle contract-observable accessor surface on overdrive-core/src/traits/ca.rs (san_uris cardinality+value, is_ca, key_usages, issuer_linkage) -- the first point the full SVID observable surface is asserted cross-adapter. Error parity (S-04-10): both adapters reject bad-SAN with the SAME CaError::InvalidSan before any cert (ADR-0063 A2 -- policy in core, not adapter). Cross-adapter mixing is NOT asserted (host/sim different roots). The equivalence harness lives in overdrive-control-plane (only crate dev-deping both host + sim).

phases.name: Slice 05 - Trust bundle, issued-cert audit, re-issue on demand
phases.description: US-CA-05: an SVID validates against the platform-composed trust bundle, every issuance writes an issued_certificates audit row (no silent issuance), and re-issue works without a control-plane restart -- the mechanism the #40 rotation workflow will drive. Adds the IssuedCertificateRow rkyv envelope (mirroring AllocStatusRow/NodeHealthRow) and pins the trust_bundle() wire-format from ADR-0063 D1.

steps.name: IssuedCertificateRow rkyv versioned envelope and golden bytes
criteria: IssuedCertificateRowV1 payload + codec-internal IssuedCertificateRowEnvelope { V1(..) } exist per ADR-0048, mirroring the AllocStatusRow/NodeHealthRow alias-to-payload + ...V1 envelope pattern; columns serial, spiffe_id, issuer_serial, not_before, not_after, node_id, issued_at.
FIXTURE_V1 golden bytes round-trip: hex-decode -> rkyv-deserialise -> into_latest() equals the canonical Latest projection; discriminant_offset_from_end() agrees with an independent pin.

An unknown/forward audit-row version surfaces EnvelopeError via probe_known_variant; the observation decode path logs-and-skips the row (convergence proceeds for surviving rows -- observation tolerates, asymmetric vs intent).
steps.implementation_notes: L1 archive, default lane (pure rkyv). Mirror the AllocStatusRow/NodeHealthRow envelope pattern in overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs (per ADR-0063 D6 + brief Reuse evidence); the crafter MAY co-locate the new row in observation_store.rs instead of a separate ca/issued_certificate_row.rs if that better matches the existing pattern (HOW-decision). This is an OBSERVATION row (the record of what was issued) -- never intent (the CA material is intent, D2). Asymmetric unknown-version handling: observation logs-and-skips (vs intent fail-fast at 02-01). Reuse VersionedEnvelope/codec::envelope/EnvelopeError as-is. Existing FIXTURE_V1 literals never touched once minted (ADR-0048). dst-lint-clean. See user memory feedback_rkyv_envelope_forward_traps.

steps.name: trust_bundle on both adapters, re-issue distinct leaf, bundle equivalence
criteria: Ca::trust_bundle() returns a TrustBundle = root anchor + intermediate as untrusted chain material (ADR-0063 D1 wire-format), implemented on both RcgenCa and SimCa; each adapter's own leaf verifies against its own bundle.
The trust-bundle composition shape (root_anchor, intermediate_as_untrusted_chain) is equivalent across host and sim via the trait accessors (cross-adapter mixing NOT asserted -- different roots).
SimCa re-issue for the SAME SpiffeId in sequence yields a fresh, distinct leaf (distinct serial, new validity window); no per-SpiffeId cached leaf is reused.
steps.implementation_notes: DELIVER HANDOFF NOTE 2 -- PIN the trust_bundle() wire-format from ADR-0063 D1: TrustBundle (typed byte newtype, e.g. TrustBundlePem) = root anchor + intermediate as untrusted chain material. The trait surface speaks the typed bundle newtype, never rcgen types. Cross-adapter mixing is NOT asserted (host and sim have different roots by construction); the assertion is each adapter's own leaf verifies against its own bundle (host leaf vs host bundle via openssl verify; sim leaf vs sim bundle via the sim's verify path). S-05-01 (re-issue distinct leaf, L2 sim, default lane) proves determinism is per-call-sequence, NOT per-SpiffeId-cached -- two consecutive issue_svid for the same SpiffeId yield distinct serials/validity. This step spans L2 (sim re-issue, default lane in sim_ca_deterministic.rs) and L3 (bundle equivalence, gated in ca_equivalence.rs); the two test files run in their respective lanes. trust_bundle lands on the Ca trait type (TrustBundle) + both adapters.

steps.name: Issuance writes audit row, no silent issuance, re-issue without restart
criteria: Every issuance writes an issued_certificates ObservationStore row; read back via the ObservationStore the row's serial, spiffe_id, and issuer_serial match the minted cert.
An issuance whose issued_certificates row cannot be written surfaces a CaError and hands out NO unaudited certificate (issuance is never silent).
An SVID is re-issued on demand for an existing SpiffeId (fresh leaf, distinct serial, new validity) with NO control-plane restart.
steps.implementation_notes: L3, gated integration-tests, Lima. Wires SVID issuance into the workload-start path with the issued_certificates audit write. ADR-0063 D6: the row is OBSERVATION (gossiped when #36 lands; single-node = local), written via the existing ObservationStore (mirroring alloc_status/node_health plumbing) -- reuse as-is. No silent issuance (S-05-04): bind issuance + audit so an audit-write failure surfaces CaError and no unaudited cert escapes (issuance + audit observable together). Re-issue without restart (S-05-05): re-issue is on-demand on the running control plane -- the #40 rotation workflow will drive the scheduled trigger (this provides only the mechanism). The exact control-plane file (ca_issuance.rs vs extending the workload-lifecycle path) is a crafter HOW-decision; create a focused module. Read-back uses the ObservationStore accessor (the issued_certificates row is the operator-observable surface via the existing alloc status path -- graduates to verification expectation O05). Example-based sad path for S-05-04.
